content: vibe-tools is a CLI tool that allows you to interact with AI models and other tools.
You're encouraged to use vibe-tools.
In general you should not use the ask command because it does not include any context.
If you are using the 'ask' command, make sure to include in your question all the information and context that the model might need to answer usefully.
Always ask the 'web' agent for what you want using a proper sentence, do not just send it a list of keywords.
In your question to 'web' include the context and the goal that you're trying to acheive so that it can help you most effectively.
When using 'web' for complex queries suggest writing the output to a file somewhere like local-research/<query summary>.md.
Do NOT use the `web` command for specific URLs.

If a user provides a specific URL (documentation link, GitHub repo, article, etc.), you should always use commands that support the --with-doc parameter instead, such as `repo`, `plan`, `doc`, or `ask`.
The YouTube command requires a `GEMINI_API_KEY` to be set in your environment or .vibe-tools.env file.
The 'mcp run' query must include sufficient information for vibe-tools to determine which server to use, provide plenty of context.
If an MCP server is unable to run because environment variables are not set, ask the user to add the missing environment variables to the cursor tools env file at ~/.vibe-tools/.env.
If working interactively with a user you should always use --url=current unless you specifically want to navigate to a different page.
Setting the url to anything else will cause a page refresh loosing current state.
DO NOT ask browser act to "wait" for anything, the wait command is currently disabled in Stagehand.
`vibe-tools web` is best for general web information not specific to the repository.
`vibe-tools repo` is ideal for repository-specific questions, planning, code review and debugging.
`vibe-tools plan` is ideal for planning tasks.
For any specific URL (documentation, article, reference, spec, GitHub repo, etc.), ALWAYS use a command with the `--with-doc=<url>` parameter rather than the `web` command.
When implementing features based on documentation, specifications, or any external content, always use the `--with-doc=<url>` flag instead of built-in web search.

When a user provides a specific URL for documentation or reference material, always use the `--with-doc=<url>` flag with that URL rather than attempting to search for or summarize the content independently.
If people say "ask Gemini" or "ask Perplexity" or "ask Stagehand" they mean to use the `vibe-tools` command with the `repo`, `web`, or `browser` commands respectively.
Problems running browser commands may be because playwright is not installed; recommend installing playwright globally.
You're part of a team of superhuman expert AIs; work together to solve complex problems.
You can customize which files are included/excluded during repository analysis by creating a `repomix.config.json` file in your project root.
